Job Title: Senior Dynamics 365 CE Technical Consultant - Customer Insights
Ad Text:
We are working with a Microsoft consulting practice that is continuing to scale its customer engagement capability and is looking to hire a Senior Technial Consultant specialising in Dynamics 365 Customer Insights and Marketing.

This role offers exposure to a high volume of project work, a mix of established clients and new greenfield implementations, and a clear pathway into leadership as the practice grows.

The Role

You will work across customer data, segmentation, and marketing automation initiatives, partnering closely with clients to design and deliver modern engagement solutions.

Responsibilities will include:

Leading discovery sessions, requirements gathering, and solution design

Designing and delivering segmentation and audience strategies for targeted engagement

Implementing Dynamics 365 Marketing and Real Time Journeys

Advising on consent management, forms, events, and campaign automation

Collaborating with architects and technical teams to ensure data is fit for activation

Running client workshops and acting as a trusted advisor throughout delivery

Supporting a strong pipeline of concurrent projects across multiple industries

About You

This role suits a confident, client-facing consultant who enjoys variety and responsibility.

You may come from a Marketing, CRM, or Customer Insights background and bring:

Strong hands-on experience with Dynamics 365 Marketing and or Customer Insights

Exposure to Real Time Marketing and modern journey design

Some experience working with the Power Platform and Dataverse (deep expertise not required)

Experience delivering in a consulting or agency environment

Strong communication skills and the ability to work autonomously

Ambition to progress into a lead or leadership role over time

Why This Role

Clear and supported pathway into leadership

Consistent project flow with minimal bench time

Opportunity to influence solution design and delivery approach

Collaborative team within a broader Power Platform practice

Location

Sydney preferred

Hybrid working environment

If you're looking for a senior consulting role with real ownership, ongoing project work, and long-term progression, we'd like to speak with you.

The Opportunity

This role is perfect for someone who enjoys being hands‑on and playing a key role in the smooth running of investment governance and supplier management. You’ll be joining a mature, well‑structured environment where frameworks, policies and automation are already established—allowing you to focus on delivery, relationships and continuous improvement.

You’ll thrive here if you learn quickly, take initiative and feel confident engaging with external investment managers, internal stakeholders and contract owners. Strong communication skills, curiosity and the ability to influence across teams will set you up for success. While the technical governance elements can be developed on the job, experience in regulatory risk, compliance, legal or investment operations will help you hit the ground running.

How You'll Make an Impact

Support the upkeep and ongoing enhancement of investment governance processes, policies and dashboards, including contributing to automation initiatives.

Manage day‑to‑day interactions with external investment managers and key investment suppliers.

Assist in maintaining the operational due diligence framework, ensuring documentation and processes remain accurate and up to date.

Contribute to contract updates and IMA maintenance as external investment management services transition into the business.

About You

To be successful in this role, you bring:

Experience in regulatory risk, compliance, legal, or investment operations.

Exposure to insurance, superannuation or funds management (advantageous but not essential).

Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to influence and build productive relationships.

Familiarity with investment or risk policies within an asset‑owner environment.

Industry qualifications such as CFA (or progression toward it) are valued but not required.

This permanent role offers the chance to grow your capability while making a meaningful impact from day one. If you enjoy ownership, relationship‑building and working across policy maintenance, contract updates and supplier oversight, this opportunity provides a strong platform to develop within a collaborative, purpose‑driven investment governance team.

About Us 

Automic Group is Australia’s market leader in technology-driven investor administration. Combining proprietary cloud-native technology with expert service, Automic simplifies investor administration and governance for companies and funds. We provide the market's leading registry, employee share plans, IR analytics, and fund administration services for over 1,400 ASX-listed and unlisted organisations.  

Automic’s culture is collaborative, inclusive, and purpose driven. It is grounded in our mission to connect companies and funds to their investors through technology, service, and trust, and guided by our values of One Automic, Go Beyond, Empowered, and Accountable. 




About the Role 

The Senior Risk Manager is a senior Line 2 leadership role responsible for the continuous refinement and operationalisation of Automic Group’s Risk Management Framework. This position strengthens enterprise and operational risk capability across the Group by embedding disciplined, practical and commercially aligned risk management practices that enable growth. 

You will integrate risk appetite into decision-making, leverage technology and AI to enhance risk insights, and deliver high-quality quantitative and qualitative reporting to Executive and Board stakeholders. Working closely with business leaders, you will ensure risk management supports strategic objectives — enabling calculated risk-taking rather than constraining ambition. 

This is a high-autonomy role operating with CRO oversight and strong executive engagement. 

 

Responsibilities 

Refine and operationalise the Line 2 Risk Management Framework aligned to ISO 31000 

Embed risk appetite considerations into business processes, systems and decision-making 

Design, develop and monitor KRIs aligned to the Risk Appetite Statement 

Facilitate structured quarterly risk reviews and oversee risk registers across business units 

Strengthen operational risk practices including control assessments, incident analysis, BCM and resilience 

Apply quantitative risk methodologies (e.g., Monte Carlo, scenario modelling) to assess material exposures 

Lead implementation and optimisation of an enterprise GRC platform 

Explore and deploy AI-enabled tools for risk identification, analytics and predictive modelling 

Deliver clear, insightful Board and Executive risk reporting and dashboards 

Act as a trusted advisor to business leaders, providing constructive Line 2 challenge 

Promote and uplift risk culture through workshops, education and practical engagement 

 




Skills and Experience 

8+ years’ experience in enterprise and/or operational risk 

Strong understanding of ISO 31000 and the Three Lines Model 

Experience refining and operating a Risk Management Framework 

Demonstrated experience delivering Board and Executive-level risk reporting 

Experience developing and monitoring KRIs 

Experience facilitating structured risk workshops and register reviews 

Exposure to risk quantification techniques (Monte Carlo, scenario modelling) 

Experience implementing or managing a GRC platform 

Advanced Excel and PowerPoint capability 

Strong data analysis and dashboarding capability (e.g., Power BI) 

Commercial acumen with ability to translate risk into business impact 

Desirable: 

Professional certification (RMIA, GAICD, CRISC, FRM, CFA or equivalent) 

Experience in financial services, funds, registry or technology-enabled environments 

Exposure to AI or automation tools in analytics or reporting 

 

About You 

You are a commercially minded risk leader who understands that great risk management enables growth. You combine analytical rigour with pragmatic execution and are comfortable operating independently in a fast-paced, evolving environment. 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone to bring: 

Strong analytical and critical thinking capability 

Confidence engaging and influencing senior stakeholders 

The ability to challenge constructively while building trusted partnerships 

Clear and compelling written and verbal communication skills 

A continuous improvement mindset 

High attention to detail with strong delivery discipline 

Curiosity and enthusiasm for leveraging technology and AI in risk management 

Sound judgement in prioritising risk treatments and strategic trade-offs 

The ability to balance governance discipline with business enablement 

 

Why join Automic: 

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment. 

Key Benefits include: 

Hybrid working  

A High growth, scale-up organisation 

Professional career growth & development  

Supportive & fast paced environment 

 

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey at Automic! 

 

Applications will be reviewed as they are received and interviews may commence prior to the closing date.

Ad Text:
Your new role:

As Principal Data Scientist, you will lead the design and delivery of advanced analytics solutions that strengthen decision-making and improve healthcare outcomes across the service.  You will:

Lead end-to-end data science workflows – from data ingestion and feature engineering through to model development, deployment and optimisation.
Develop and implement advanced machine learning, statistical and predictive analytics solutions integrated into clinical and business processes.
Collaborate with clinical and executive stakeholders to define requirements and deliver dashboards, reports and analytic products aligned to customer needs.
Mentor team members and champion best practice data science methodologies, governance and standards across the organisation.
About you:

You are an experienced and strategic data science leader who thrives in complex environments and is passionate about using data to improve health system performance. You have: 

A tertiary qualification in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering or a related discipline (postgraduate qualifications highly regarded).
Strong expertise in machine learning, statistical modelling, data transformation and working with large, disparate datasets using tools such as Python, R and SQL.
Proven ability to design and deliver BI dashboards and analytic products (experience with Qlik or Power BI highly regarded).
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to translate complex data insights into meaningful outcomes for clinicians and executives.
What we can offer you:
We offer a family-friendly work environment that supports you in making the most of Queensland's laid-back lifestyle.
We are renowned for teaching and research excellence with strong relations with Queensland's leading tertiary institutions.
We want to empower you to build a long-term career, with countless opportunities to learn and grow, no matter your role
You'll have all the benefits of working for Queensland's world-class public health system including generous leave entitlements, attractive salary and remuneration packages, salary packaging and access to relevant professional development allowances and/or leave, study and research assistance, corporate discounts and employee wellness programs.
About the Data and Analytics Service

The Data & Analytics service at Metro South Health, within the Digital Health and Informatics division, plays a pivotal role in supporting evidence-based decision-making across the health network. By leveraging advanced data management, analysis, and reporting capabilities, the team transforms complex healthcare data into actionable insights that enhance patient outcomes, operational efficiency, and strategic planning. This service collaborates closely with clinical, operational, and research teams to ensure timely access to high-quality data, promote data-driven innovation, and support continuous improvement initiatives across Metro South Health.

INTEGRITY | COMPASSION | ACCOUNTABILITY | RESPECT | ENGAGEMENT | EXCELLENCE

Our values were created by employees for employees to shape our culture and inspire positive interactions in the workplace. Diversity of ideas, skills, traditions, and customs is celebrated as one of our greatest strengths. Having a workforce that reflects and understands the needs and expectations of our community is important to delivering safe, kinder, and more inclusive care.

We recognise our strength comes from the diversity of our people and so we encourage people of all genders, ethnicities, ages, abilities, languages, sexual orientation, and family responsibilities to apply.

We are Metro South Health:

We are the major public healthcare provider for Brisbane's south side, Logan, Redlands, and the Scenic Rim operating five major hospitals and a range of community, specialty, and state-wide healthcare services.

We are bold, innovative, collaborative, inspiring. We save lives, change lives, and make the world a better place. We are part of and proud of the community we serve.

We are better together.

About the role:

What you'll do

What You’ll Enjoy

Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging ML best practices, continuously evolving the domain to meet Canva’s scale and ambition.

You're probably a match if

You’re passionate about coaching and developing engineers, creating a culture of growth and support.

You’re experienced in designing systems at scale, with a focus on reliability, performance, and extensibility.

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

You have experience leading an engineering team in a Product or SaaS environment, ideally within ML or applied AI domains.

Prior experience in text, image, video, and audio understanding is highly desirable.

Additional Information


What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too

Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers

An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more

Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

About us 
We are an award winning, national $16B public offer industry fund focused on the education and community sectors. Working for NGS Super means being part of something bigger and working to make a difference to our members and their financial future.  

Our people are key to our success, and as we expand, we’re committed to finding and retaining the right talent to take on the journey.  As well as a flexible and fun workplace, we offer competitive benefits including additional leave entitlements, personal & professional development and health & wellbeing programs. 

Click on the video below to learn more! 

The Role 
Working closely with the Senior Manager, Responsible Investment, you’ll deliver high quality ESG research, analysis, reporting and engagement to support informed investment decisions.
The role spans policy and ESG strategy, research, investment selection and monitoring, and active ownership—requiring strong technical expertise, analytical insight and hands on execution to advance the Fund’s responsible investment objectives.

What you’ll do

Policy and Strategy: Support the maintenance and enhancement of the Fund’s responsible investment framework, policies and procedures. Ensure team awareness of policy updates. Undertake research to identify emerging ESG issues, regulatory changes and industry best practice, and provide recommendations to the Senior Manager. 
Research & Implementation: Conduct research on ESG themes, sector-level trends, controversies, standards and best practice to inform decision making. Prepare well structured ESG thematic papers, manager briefing notes, and Investment Committee inputs.  Support monitoring of the investment portfolio for ESG risks and opportunities, including assessment of data platforms, external ratings and stewardship inputs. 
Selection, Monitoring and Reporting: Complete ESG due diligence on investment opportunities, including data gathering, scoring, documentation and preparation of reports. Support the ESG compliance review process and summarise findings for internal stakeholders. Prepare internal ESG dashboards, metrics and reporting, ensuring data integrity and alignment with governance requirements. 
Engagement: Support internal and external engagement activities, including active ownership, stewardship, and proxy voting. Assist with the monitoring of proxy voting outcomes and prepare summaries for the Senior Manager. Coordinate engagement actions with external managers and stewardship partners, tracking progress and outcomes. 
Cross-functional ESG initiatives: Provide analytical and coordination support for organisation wide ESG initiatives (e.g., AASB S2, climate strategy, modern slavery, biodiversity etc). Collaborate as needed with other teams to ensure ESG related workstreams are delivered on time and to required standards. 

About you 
With a strong understanding of responsible investment and ESG integration concepts across asset classes, you will bring the following key skills:

Demonstrated analytical capability, including ability to interpret ESG data, identify issues, and prepare high quality written outputs. 
Working knowledge of ESG standards and frameworks (e.g., PRI, TCFD/IFRS S2, SASB, GHG Protocol). 
Experience contributing to ESG processes such as due diligence, monitoring, reporting or engagement. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate analysis into clear insights. 
Strong stakeholder collaboration skills. 
Qualifications at an appropriate level in a relevant discipline (e.g., Finance, Economics, Law, Sustainability, Commerce) or demonstrated equivalent experience.

The following skills are nice to have but not essential:

Experience in climate risk analysis, scenario modelling or portfolio level climate assessments. 
Familiarity with biodiversity and nature related risks. 
Experience supporting ESG disclosures to Boards, regulators or members. 
Experience with ESG data platforms, ratings providers or analytical tools. 
Experience within a superannuation fund, institutional investor or asset manager. 
Relevant postgraduate study or professional certification (e.g., CFA, ESG certifications).

If you have high levels of professional judgement, integrity and accountability, with a strong member-first mindset, would love to hear from you.

We are a super fund that has an exceptional work culture, provides a diverse offering in developing our people and you can be a part of it while earning an attractive remuneration package! 

Interested and suitably qualified candidates should submit a copy of their updated CV. 

Please note that to be eligible for this role, you are required to have permanent Australian working rights and residency. 

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a workplace that values diversity, equity, and respect for all individuals.

About the team

Expect to face intellectual challenges daily, as you delve into intricate policy issues and analyse information to provide valuable insights.

Given the ever-evolving nature of geopolitics, you can expect a fast-paced work environment. That means staying up to date with current events and adapting to new changes quickly.

The team values collaboration. You’ll engage in lively discussions, brainstorming sessions, and knowledge sharing.

The roles

ONI is recruiting for multiple current and possible future analyst vacancies at the EL2 classification in our Intelligence Assessments Divisions.

Our Intelligence Analysts apply high level analytical tradecraft and research acumen to generate assessments and briefings on matters of political, strategic and economic significance to Australia. Our analysts help inform national security decision making by providing the Prime Minister and other members of the National Security Committee of Cabinet with intelligence-based advice, analysis and assessments.

Our analysts specialise in delivering insight by rigorously synthesising, distilling and analysing information to meet our customers’ needs. We draw on all sources of information available to the Australian Government including material covertly collected by other intelligence agencies, diplomatic reporting, open-source intelligence, and insights we gather from talking to world-leading experts in business, academia and think-tanks.

As an EL2 team member you will:

Perform an important leadership role, developing and delivering strategic, policies, priorities and operational practices in support of ONI’s objectives.
Plan, develop, edit and deliver strategic intelligence products that ensure ONI has policy impact, through analytical rigour, effective prioritisation and sound business processes.
Deliver specialist briefings to senior stakeholders and provide timely input to policy processes.
Cultivate, establish and maintain strong working relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders across government, with external experts, and with foreign partners.
Represent and negotiate highly complex issues on behalf of ONI at, a variety of internal and external meetings and working groups.
Foster ONI’s analytical principles and standards, and  provide direction to develop staff and analytical capability.
Contribute to ONI’s leadership of the National Intelligence Community.
Who are we looking for?

We recruit staff with a wide range of backgrounds, skill levels, disciplines and working styles. We look for aptitude and insight, rather than time served in similar roles. We welcome applications from individuals at all career stages and from any sector. A commonality all our analysts have is a deep interest in helping to protect people and places.

We have a specific interest in candidates with backgrounds in the following areas:

Thematic – Economics, Trade, Climate and Energy, Terrorism, Transnational Crime, Irregular Migration, Military Capability, Major Power Dynamics, Strategic Outlook, Foreign Interference.
Geopolitical – Pacific, Southeast Asia, China, Europe, Africa, Middle East, India, Japan/Koreas, America.
Critical Technology and Cyber – including Artificial Intelligence, quantum, biotech and space.

Your experience and knowledge may have been developed while working for the Australian government in a diplomatic, policy or intelligence role. Or you may have a think tank, academic, private sector or other relevant background. 

At the EL2 classification we look for deep and broad subject matter expertise, a strong ability to exercise astute decision making, and an aptitude for coordinating and collaborating with other NIC agencies. Our EL2s also play a strong role in building organisational capability and exercising leadership. For more information on the APS Work Level Standards, please visit the APSC website.

Key attributes

To be successful in this role you will need to demonstrate:

Experience in a leadership role, with the ability to develop analytical capability.
Advanced subject matter knowledge with a well-developed understanding of the strategic implications for Australia.
Demonstrated experience in integrated analysis, evaluating contested information and reaching robust conclusions.
Advanced written and verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate clearly and persuasively.
Excellent interpersonal and representational skills, with the ability to build strong internal and external relationships and to represent ONI in the context of highly complex and sensitive issues.
Proven capacity to manage collaborative projects, set priorities and evaluate outcomes, both internally and with other agencies.

Desirable:

Demonstrated ability to set the strategic direction for a team whilst actively managing resources and staff.

For further information about these opportunities and how to apply, please download a candidate information kit from the careers page of ONI's website.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML and AI
Ad Text:
This is: the job
You’ll join a high-performing Data Science team at the centre of a growing AI and machine learning capability within a large, enterprise environment. This is a 12 month contract based in Melbourne, focused on delivering advanced analytics and production grade Machine Learning solutions that drive measurable commercial impact.
As the Senior Data Scientist, you’ll take ownership of designing, building, and deploying scalable machine learning models using Azure Machine Learning. You’ll operate across the full ML lifecycle, from proof of concept through to production, ensuring solutions are robust, reliable, and aligned to business outcomes. Success in this role means delivering models that don’t just work in theory but create real value in-market.

This is: what you’ll do
Partner with stakeholders to identify high-value AI/ML use cases and define measurable success metrics
Design, develop, and deploy end to end machine learning solutions using Azure ML
Lead rapid proof of concept development to validate feasibility and business impact
Own feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, validation, and performance optimisation
Implement and enhance MLOps practices, including CI/CD, automated monitoring, drift detection, and retraining
Build and maintain scalable ML pipelines for batch and real time inference via Azure ML Endpoints
Collaborate with data engineers and software engineers to integrate feature stores and production grade data pipelines
Lead code reviews and contribute to solution design workshops to uplift technical standards
Present insights, demos, and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Mentor and support other data scientists, fostering a strong culture of learning and technical excellence

This is: what you’ll need
5+ years’ hands-on experience delivering ML models from development through to production
Strong expertise in Python and SQL (R or other languages for experimentation advantageous)
Deep experience with Azure Machine Learning, Pipelines, Data Factory, and MLOps frameworks
Proven capability across feature engineering, model evaluation, optimisation, and lifecycle management
Experience implementing CI/CD for ML, automated retraining strategies, and cloud-based ML infrastructure
Demonstrated success delivering and iterating on proof-of-concepts in fast-paced environments
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate complex analytics into business value
Exposure to recommendation systems, time series forecasting, or geospatial analytics (desirable)

This is: the perks
Work on large-scale, enterprise AI initiatives with regional impact
Influence architecture and ML standards across multiple business domains
Access to cutting-edge tooling and high-performance hardware

This is: for good
Apply with purpose. Be part of a placement that gives back. For every role we fill, we donate to a charity of the client’s choice. We’ve surpassed $1 million donated and we’re just getting started.

This is: what’s next
Apply now or reach out to:
Leanne O’Connor
Director, Natural Selection Group
M: 0402 145 774
E: leanne.oconnor@naturalselectiongroup.com.au

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - Controlling (18 month Fixed Term Contract)
Ad Text:
About Miele 

What does it take for a family-run company to become world market leader in the premium domestic appliance segment? It takes more than offering average products, run-of-the-mill service or short-sighted actions. It also requires the aspiration to do it forever better and to be forever better. This stance accounts for a good working environment at Miele and the success of our products. Our colleagues can rest assured that those who live up to our standards of quality and professionalism grow personally and professionally. No matter if they are apprentices or managers. 

Make our claim your challenge - whether in Gütersloh, Melbourne or Singapore! Become one of more than 23,000 Miele employees in 50 countries around the world who are committed to making our products even better every day - and help us to expand our innovation leadership.

About Your Workplace

Reporting to the Head of Controlling, this Senior Financial Analyst role is a key strategic partner within the organisation, providing insightful analysis and performance reporting that supports decision-making and drives sustainable growth across Miele Australia.

Your Responsibilities

• Act as the primary finance business partner to Marketing and Product divisions, delivering strategic insights and actionable recommendations.
• Provide forward-looking financial analysis to identify risks, opportunities, and market growth initiatives.
• Develop detailed profitability analysis by customer, channel, and product to support informed commercial decisions.
• Lead budgeting and forecasting processes for Marketing and Product, including preparation and submission of corporate templates.
• Deliver scenario modelling for new product introductions, promotions, and commission structures to optimise returns.
• Oversee month-end close activities for Marketing and Product, ensuring accuracy and integrity of results.
• Maintain and enhance reporting tools (Cognos, Data Warehouse, Power BI), ensuring data accuracy and delivering meaningful performance dashboards.

What We Are Looking For

Must-have:
• CA or CPA qualification with strong management accounting and financial analysis capability
• Advanced analytical and financial modelling skills with the ability to translate data into commercial insights
• Strong ERP and business systems experience (SAP, AS400, Cognos or similar)
• Proven ability to partner with stakeholders and influence decision-making across functions
• Excellent Excel and reporting skills with strong attention to detail and reconciliation capability

Nice-to-have:
• Experience in Retail, Distribution or FMCG environments
• Exposure to Power BI, Business Objects or other data visualisation tools
• Experience driving process improvements and acting as a change agent
• Strong understanding of profitability analysis across product portfolios

Our Benefits

• Flexible working hours
• Personal development and career opportunities
• International development programs
• Access to a wide range of wellbeing programs, including annual flu vaccinations and employee assistance support
• Experience meaningful recognition for both individual accomplishments and team successes

At Cotality, we are driven by a single mission—to make the property industry faster, smarter, and more people-centric. Cotality is the trusted source for property intelligence, with unmatched precision, depth, breadth, and insights across the entire ecosystem. Our talented team of 5,000 employees globally uses our network, scale, connectivity and technology to drive the largest asset class in the world. Join us as we work toward our vision of fueling a thriving global property ecosystem and a more resilient society.

Cotality is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive work culture that inspires innovation and bold thinking; it's a place where you can collaborate, feel valued, develop skills and directly impact the real estate economy. We know our people are our greatest asset. At Cotality, you can be yourself, lift people up and make an impact. By putting clients first and continuously innovating, we're working together to set the pace for unlocking new possibilities that better serve the property industry.

Job Description:

We are seeking a Principal, Performance Excellence to be the engine of enterprise-wide operational efficiency and strategic capacity creation. This role is pivotal in leveraging technologies like AI, digitisation, and automation to fundamentally redesign core business processes, enabling the entire organisation to achieve new levels of productivity, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

You are a visionary and pragmatic person of influence who thrives on transforming complex operational challenges into streamlined, automated solutions. Reporting to the VP, Enterprise Operations, you will have significant decision-making authority and the autonomy to select transformation projects, recommend technology investments, and set governance standards across the company. If you possess a unique blend of strategic foresight and a relentless focus on measurable profitability improvement, we want to hear from you.

What You Will Be Doing:


Design and implement a holistic, multi-year transformation roadmap focused on process automation and enterprise-wide optimisation, whilst focusing on in-year results.

Establish and govern a profitability and margin expansion centre of excellence (CoE), defining standards for security, scalability, and delivery.

Analyse complex, end-to-end business processes using data to pinpoint friction points, bottlenecks, and automation opportunities.

Lead discovery workshops with business leaders to translate strategic performance goals into tangible automation and AI project requirements.

Oversee the development and deployment lifecycle for all automation, digitisation, and AI solutions, ensuring effective planning and execution.

Measure and report on validated ROI, capacity created, and financial tracking associated with the automation pipeline.

Coach and mentor a high-performing team of specialists, fostering technical excellence and business acumen within the CoE.

Champion the integration of data analytics and process mining to continuously monitor automated processes and identify next-generation improvements.

Job Qualifications:

What You Will Bring:


Essential:

5+ years of progressive experience leading large-scale process improvement and digital transformation programs.

Certification in Lean or an equivalent advanced process methodology.

Hands-on experience designing and managing solutions utilising RPA, AI/ML, and intelligent document processing (IDP).

Executive presence and storytelling skills to translate complex technical concepts into compelling business cases for the executive suite.

A "Challenger Mindset" that naturally questions the status quo and sees technology as a lever for business reinvention.

Demonstrated experience managing a project portfolio and collaborating with technical teams of developers or engineers.

Bias for action with the grit to see large-scale programs through to measurable completion.

Preferred:

Cross-functional acumen to discuss both high-level business strategy and technical API integration.

Experience with enterprise systems such as Salesforce and Zendesk.

Familiarity with process mining tools and data analytics integration.

Why Join Cotality?

Cotality has a culture of collaboration- we champion ownership, initiative and leadership! To support your whole self, our benefits are built to help you thrive at work and in life.

In addition to modern offices across Australia and New Zealand, our hybrid working model provides you with the opportunity for flexible working conditions that work best for you and the business;
10 half days of Wellbeing leave each year to help you recharge and access to our Employee Assistance Program;
$600 AUD annual wellbeing allowance for gym memberships, wellness apps, ergonomic gear and more;
Generous Parental leave offering, $750 AUD in Baby Bucks and our peer recognition turned into rewards program - Bravo!
Free subscription to the Calm app as well as other mindfulness tools, online health and nutrition courses;
Complimentary access to Australia’s most comprehensive property research platforms including RP Data and exclusive employee-only market updates and access to the industry’s best property experts;
Two community and volunteer leave days each year to support meaningful causes and our charity partners.

Cotality's Diversity Commitment:​

Cotality is fully committed to employing a diverse workforce and creating an inclusive work environment that embraces everyone’s unique contributions, experiences and values. We offer an empowered work environment that encourages creativity, initiative and professional growth and provides a competitive salary and benefits package. ​ We are better together when we support and recognize our differences. 

​

Equal Opportunity Employer Statement:​

Cotality is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and respectful workplace across Australia and New Zealand. We recruit, hire, train, and promote individuals based on merit, qualifications, and business needs, without regard to race, ethnicity, colour, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and maintaining a drug-free workplace free from discrimination, harassment, and bullying.  ​

​

Please apply on our website for consideration.​

Privacy Policy

Global Applicant Privacy Policy

By providing your telephone number, you agree to receive automated (SMS) text messages at that number from Cotality regarding all matters related to your application and, if you are hired, your employment and company business. Message & data rates may apply. You can opt out at any time by responding STOP or UNSUBSCRIBING and will automatically be opted out company-wide.

Connect with us on social media!  Click on the quicklinks below to find out more about our company and associates

Company: Sharp & Carter Technology, Projects & Digital Brisbane
Ad Text:
We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support a Digital & Enterprise Applications function within a large, complex organisation undergoing significant transformation.
 
This role will play a key part in strengthening governance and audit readiness across critical Finance and HR enterprise platforms, recently transitioned into technology ownership. With external audits scheduled in the coming quarters, the focus is on ensuring policies, procedures and controls are clearly defined, documented, accessible and aligned to governance requirements.
 
The Opportunity:
Reporting to the Head of Digital & Enterprise Applications, you will:

Review existing enterprise policies and governance frameworks
Interpret requirements relating to technical application ownership
Conduct a gap analysis (current state vs required state)
Identify missing, outdated or incomplete artefacts
Develop an actionable remediation plan
Support the creation and uplift of key documentation and procedures
Ensure strong audit traceability (clear storage, documentation and evidence pathways)
Collaborate with platform SMEs where required
The objective is to ensure the organisation can confidently demonstrate appropriate governance, particularly in relation to user access management, onboarding/offboarding processes and security controls across audited platforms.
 
What We're Looking For:
This is not a deeply technical platform role. Instead, we are seeking a strong, pragmatic and adaptable BA who can apply an audit lens to governance and process.
 
Key experience includes:

Proven Business Analysis experience across enterprise environments
Strong documentation and gap analysis capability
Experience interpreting and applying policy and governance frameworks
Exposure to audit environments or controls-based work (highly desirable)
Understanding of user access management processes and control frameworks
Experience working with Finance or HR platforms (desirable, not essential)
Ability to work autonomously, search for and assess existing artefacts (e.g. SharePoint repositories) and identify gaps
You will be detail-oriented, structured in your thinking and comfortable navigating ambiguity while driving outcomes.
 
For more information please reach out to Max at mpower@sharpandcarter.com.au

The Pricing & Services Analyst is responsible for the provision of specialist and strategic analysis and insights on costing, pricing and strategic performance for Silverchain. The role will lead the definition, development, and implementation of best practice solutions to support modelling of required pricing and margin outcomes on specific contract tender submissions and business performance initiatives.

The role holder ensures that governance standards, tools and processes can be applied to pricing and costing tasks for delivery to the State Executives and Commercial teams. The role will work with the Group Executive, Commercial, Business Development and Finance teams to provide robust analysis and advice on performance, costing of services and the development and promotion of initiatives to increase efficiencies, optimise cost recovery and improve product viability.

What's on offer

Fulltime permanent position Monday-Friday 76 hours per fortnight.
Hybrid role - working from home, flexible location VIC, WA, SA or NSW.
Working for a non-profit organisation, you can salary package up to $18,500 tax free.
Everyday discounts, including discounted vouchers for Coles, Woolworths, Amazon, Myer, The Good Guys and more.
Comprehensive health and wellbeing resources and support.

What we're looking for

To thrive in this role, you'll need to have:

Desirable to have 5+ year's experience in a comparable analyst role in aged-care, health and/or pricing/costing field.
Bachelors or Master's degree in one of the following: Business, Leadership, Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data Science, Economics, Engineering, Statistics or Mathematics.
Proven ability to deliver insights that demonstrate business value that have improved business processes / efficiencies / increased revenue etc.
Experience in working with large and complex datasets to draw out insights.
Experience in developing robust cost and price modelling practices, standards and frameworks desirable.
Accounting, finance or pricing certification is desirable, but not essential.

The role

Support costing and pricing analysis and processes.
Adhere to cost and price modelling practices, standards and frameworks and ensure they are embedded into all Silverchain services.
Transform data and information into strategic performance, pricing and costing insights that inform high level strategy and tactical decision-making.
Develop transparent benchmarking of services using contemporary data driven methodologies.
Produce analytic tools to effect business process changes to support an efficient and cost-effective, and data informed business operations environment.
Ensure the privacy and security of data assets are maintained in accordance with all legislative requirements and regulations and meets the expectations of key stakeholders.
Ensure an agile and disciplined approach to delivering outcomes for the business in collaboration with key stakeholders.

Why choose Silverchain

Everyone at Silverchain, from our care teams to leadership, plays a vital role in delivering Best Care. It's a team effort. Best Care is care that is safe, personal, connected and effective.

We are proudly one of the few Australian home care providers accredited in both national health and aged care standards. This means the quality of care we provide is expected to meet the same standards as a hospital or residential aged care facility. As a not-for-profit organisation, we re-invest our funds into research and developing innovative models of care to set the industry benchmark for the provision of the best care in the home.

We'll also provide you with opportunities to help you develop and grow in your career, with training and education programs to help you achieve your goals

How to apply

To submit your application, click ‘Apply'.

At Silverchain, we recognise the positive contribution that a diverse workforce makes to our organisation where individuals can be authentic, are respected and celebrated. We strongly encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people of all ages, gender, abilities, culturally diverse and linguistic backgrounds.

This is an opportunity for experienced consultants who want to move beyond the traditional consulting model and work in a more senior, intellectually rigorous environment.

Rather than operating through large delivery teams and leverage-heavy structures, the firm deploys small, highly experienced teams to work directly with senior government leaders on complex strategic issues.

Client Details

We are partnering with a highly respected, senior-led advisory firm that works directly with Australian federal and state governments on some of the country's most complex and nationally significant policy challenges.

Description

As a Management Consultant, you will play a key role in delivering high-impact advisory work for senior government stakeholders.

Responsibilities will include:

Leading structured problem-solving across complex policy and strategic challenges
Conducting economic, policy and regulatory analysis
Developing strategic recommendations and advisory reports for senior decision-makers
Supporting the design and implementation of major government initiatives
Engaging directly with departmental leadership and senior policy stakeholders
Working closely with highly experienced advisors including former departmental and defence leaders
Unlike large consulting firms, you will have direct exposure to clients and senior decision-makers from day one.

Profile

This role is well suited to consultants currently working in:

Big 4 consulting firms
Tier-1 strategy firms
Public sector advisory practices

We are particularly interested in individuals with experience in:

Government and public sector consulting
Infrastructure, energy or climate policy
Economic policy and regulatory advisory
Defence or national security projects
Health or social policy reform

You will bring:

Strong analytical and structured problem-solving capability
Excellent written communication and briefing skills
Ability to operate comfortably in ambiguous and complex environments
A genuine interest in shaping public policy outcomes

Job Offer

For consultants coming from larger firms, this opportunity offers:

Direct exposure to senior government decision-makers
Greater ownership of work and intellectual contribution
Less hierarchy and fewer layers of internal review
Work that directly influences national policy outcomes
A more agile, senior-led consulting environment

It is an opportunity to continue advising government - but with greater influence, deeper policy engagement, and a more meaningful consulting model.

To apply online please click the 'Apply' button below. For a confidential discussion about this role please contact Ben Naylor on +61386166225.

About the Role

In this position, you will work closely with your manager to:

Support the design and development of Power BI dashboards and reports that present agreed KPIs, SLAs and service performance measures across shared services.
Support the development of an integrated Contact Centre reporting view, consolidating demand, volumes and performance across multiple channels and hand-offs.
Support the design, coordination and facilitation of workshops with stakeholders to agree on KPIs, SLAs and service performance measures as part of designing the Business Performance Management Framework for Corporate Shared Services, under the auspices of the CSS program, including meeting program timelines and deliverables.
Support the development of standardised data models to support drill-down reporting from executive-level views to transactional and service-level detail.
Partner with stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and translate them into clear data models, metrics and visualisations.
Extract, transform and prepare data from multiple source systems using SQL and Snowflake, ensuring accuracy, consistency and usability for reporting.
Perform data cleansing, validation and reconciliation to improve data quality and establish trusted datasets.
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies and issues.
Work closely with the MQIT integration and application teams to understand data flows, dependencies and system constraints.
Contribute to documentation of data definitions, business rules, assumptions and limitations to support consistent interpretation of reports.
Maintain the Data and Analytics stream delivery plan, recording progress in line with the schedule to ensure milestones and deliverables are met to the quality standard and timelines required by the CSS program.
Update daily project task cards, addressing issues as they arise and escalating any blockers to the program team for resolution.
Attend stand-up meetings with the program team and complete status and progress report in line with the program governance and reporting requirements.
Communicate with relevant stakeholders to provide updates regarding project status, and implementation risks and issues.
Contribute to the preparation of reports and presentations for program steering committees, as required.
Contribute to continuous improvement activities within the program team.



How to Apply

To be considered for this role, please submit your CV and a cover letter (not exceeding 2 pages) that demonstrates how you meet the following essential selection criteria:

Demonstrated experience in data analysis and reporting, including data extraction, transformation and visualisation.
Working knowledge of data structures and data management principles, including data cleansing and validation.
Proven experience using SQL to query and manipulate data from relational databases.
Strong capability in Excel, and experience in developing reports and dashboards using Power BI and data visualisation.
Experience working with Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouse platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.
Capacity to work independently in a fast-paced, program environment.
Excellent interpersonal and communications skills with demonstrated ability to develop and maintain effective relationships with stakeholders at all levels.



Desirable Criteria:

Experience or have an understanding of data modelling to support reporting solutions.
Experience ingesting data from multiple source systems, such as HR, Finance and IT platforms, into a cloud data platform
Ability to work with multiple data sources and systems and resolve data quality or consistency issues.
Experience collaborating with IT and integration teams to understand data pipelines, system dependencies and constraints.


IT Occupation/profession specific capabilities - SFIA Code/Skill (Level)

At least TWO of:

INAN: Analytics (Level 4)
DTAN: Data modelling and design (Level 3) VISL: Data visualisation (Level 4)
BSMO: Business modelling (Level 4)



Other Requirements:

Note: This position requires a criminal record check.


For role-specific enquiries, please email Carmelina Senese, Head of Enabling Services at carmelina.senese@mq.edu.au

This role sits within an HR led project focused on transitioning from one employee equity management platform to another. With architectural design already in place, we require a Technical BA to navigate technical delivery, document integrations, and manage internal service connections.

You will act as the bridge between architecture, technical teams, and HR stakeholders. You will take ownership of technical documentation, integration mapping, and implementation support across the target system. 

Key responsibilities

Document technical design and integration requirements.
Navigate and manage integration patterns, oversee file flows, and ensure correct folder structures are established.
Work alongside architect to progress delivery.
Support internal service connections (SSO, Splunk etc).
Ensure documentation supports implementation and ongoing operations. 
Required experience and skills

Previous experience within HR projects and/or experience with Reward/HR Systems. 
Experience operating within an HR technology context.
Strong technical understanding of integration patterns, file flows and internal service connections. 
Background in technical documentation and implementation delivery.
Ability to operate within structured project and process environments.
Experience engaging technical and business stakeholders.
Strong preference for financial services or consulting background. 
If you have the above experience, please apply!

There’s never been a more exciting time to shape the energy transition. As a Digital Innovation Engineer, you’ll play a pivotal role in transforming how an electricity distribution business operates designing and delivering scalable, data-driven solutions that improve safety, performance and customer outcomes.

About the Role

This is a highly technical, hands-on role for an engineer who thrives on solving complex operational and business challenges. You’ll identify inefficiencies, uncover data gaps and build end-to-end digital solutions that automate processes, enhance decision-making and unlock measurable value across the organisation.

From advanced analytics and AI/ML to digital twins and automation, you’ll apply modern technologies to real-world engineering problems and see your solutions in action.

What You’ll Do

Analyse operational, engineering and business processes to identify improvement opportunities and define clear technical problem statements
Design and deliver end-to-end digital solutions from data ingestion and transformation through to analytics, automation and visualisation
Build dashboards, analytics tools and decision-support systems that drive operational performance, safety and productivity
Develop automation solutions to eliminate manual processes and improve data quality and workflow reliability
Apply emerging technologies (Digital Twins, AI/ML, advanced analytics, robotics) to practical engineering challenges
Think outside the box with innovative and creative solutions as well as strong problem solving skills
Partner with stakeholders to understand requirements, validate assumptions and iteratively deliver business value
Continuously improve digital products based on performance insights and user feedback
Establish reusable tools, standards and patterns to uplift digital capability across the organisation
Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies and industry innovation

About You

You’re analytical, commercially aware and naturally curious. You enjoy breaking down complex problems, working across disciplines and influencing others to embrace smarter ways of working.

You bring:

3–10 years’ experience in a similar professional or technical role
A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Data Science or a related discipline
A current C Class driver licence (up to 4.5t GVM)

Technical capability in:

SQL, Python, Azure Analytics
Power BI, Knime, FME or similar analytics platforms
Data modelling, automation and workflow design

You’ll also demonstrate:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, applying innovative solutions
The ability to manage multiple concurrent projects
Clear and persuasive communication skills to handle stakeholder management
Commercial awareness and understanding of enterprise impacts
A solid understanding of electricity/energy utility operations

Why Join Us?

Work on meaningful projects that power communities
Deliver innovative solutions with real operational impact
Collaborate with engineers, analysts and business leaders
Help shape the digital future of energy

If you’re passionate about combining engineering, data and innovation to solve real-world problems — we’d love to hear from you.

For further information, please contact recruitment@endeavourenergy.com.au

Applications close – 18 March 2026. 

When we power together, we can achieve so much more.

At Endeavour Energy, having a diverse and inclusive workplace makes us better, safer, and stronger. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels safe, motivated, valued, included and respected.

Endeavour Energy is a critical infrastructure organisation and performs various background checks to evaluate the suitability of an applicant before making an employment offer to ensure the security and protection of its assets.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Our client is seeking an experienced Business Analyst - Identity & Access Management (IDAM) to join a large, complex enterprise environment undertaking a significant identity transformation program. This is initial a 3-month contract with possibility to extent.

This role will play a critical part in the discovery, design, and implementation of an Identity Access Management (IAM) solution, ensuring alignment between business objectives, security requirements, and technical delivery.
As the IDAM Business Analyst, you will bridge business and technical teams to define, document, and support the implementation of IAM capabilities including Identity Governance, Access Management, and Privileged Access Management.

Key Responsibilities

Gather, document, and analyse business requirements for IAM solutions
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder sessions across business and technical teams
Develop functional specifications, use cases, and process flows
Define user provisioning, authentication, authorisation, and role-based access controls
Document integration points between identity platforms (e.g. Active Directory / Entra ID) and enterprise systems
Support testing, validation, and post-implementation activities
Ensure compliance with security, privacy, and regulatory standards
Identify risks and drive continuous improvement across identity processes


Requirements

Minimum 5+ years' experience as a Business Analyst in complex enterprise environments
Proven experience working on Identity & Access Management (IAM / IDAM) programs
Strong understanding of IGA, Access Management (AM), and Privileged Access Management (PAM)
Experience with Active Directory and/or Microsoft Entra ID
Strong stakeholder engagement and workshop facilitation skills
Experience working in Agile delivery environments
Excellent documentation and communication capability

If you would like to know more, please contact Milly Kerei-Keepa on 0476865411/milly.kerei-keepa@talentinternational.com or hit Apply Now!

Overview


Trading Systems Project manager / Business Analysts at Susquehanna work directly with developers, technology managers, traders, and business domain experts to help drive large technology projects for one of the core businesses within our firm.

It is a multi-faceted role where you will utilize your well-developed business analyst and project management skill set to drive significant projects within a fast moving and sophisticated technology-driven environment. You will be working with in a collaborative and friendly environment with talented colleagues to drive projects end to end, from concept to go-live, guiding relevant teams through the entire project life cycle.

Role / Responsibilities :

· In this role you will be responsible for driving the delivery of trading systems projects that cross regions, teams, and domains.

· The projects are for the Asian Trading businesses and you will partner with the global teams to help deliver. Strong knowledge / experience with large and complex trading systems.

· Accomplished collaborator who can work across business teams and to assess, understand, implement, and provide guidance on requirements.

· Great communicator who effectively report on project status to all levels of staff and management regularly to increase project transparency for all participants and stakeholders.

· Strong orchestration and negotiation skills

· Ability to understand and assess probabilistic outcomes to manage risk.

· Evaluate project requirements and objectively analyse them to choose appropriate strategies, tools and/or project management methodologies.

· Curious with a desire to understand, analyse, diagram and describe complex distributed systems, processes and high-level data flows

· Flexibility to take on projects at short notice, as well as handle multiple projects with varying deadlines.

· Able to adapt in a changeable environment and familiar with agile mythologies.

What you can expect from us:

Real Impact: By integrating sophisticated coding techniques with innovative engineering ideas, we design and optimize systems that can process massive amounts of data while still ensuring high performance and stability. You’ll see how your contributions towards developing and supporting leading-edge hardware and software technologies make a firm-wide impact that makes us all smarter, faster, and better.

Collaboration: You will partner closely with our Infrastructure, Project Management and Strategy Developer Teams to deliver and manage scalable and highly performant systems to support trading in the region and globally.

Growth: For many of our roles, we don’t expect you to have prior industry experience in proprietary trading or financial services to succeed at Susquehanna International Group. We’re looking for people who are naturally curious, relentless problem solvers, and have the desire to continuously innovate, learn, and grow.

Benefits: Susquehanna offers a wide array of competitive employee perks & benefits

What we're looking for


· Minimum of 5 years of experience with the delivering trading systems.

· Minimum of 5 years of experience in a Business Analysis (BA), Project Management (PM) or BA/PM role in IT environment with management of SDLC projects.

· Great communicator who effectively report on project status to all levels of staff and management regularly to increase project transparency for all participants and stakeholders.

· Strong negotiation skills and the ability to understand and assess probabilistic outcomes to manage risk.

· Bachelor’s degree or similar experience level in a technical discipline

Equal Opportunity Statement

We encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds, and we welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process to ensure that you can best demonstrate your abilities.

About Susquehanna

Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together.

What we do

We are experts in trading essentially all listed financial products and asset classes, with a focus on derivatives trading. Through market making and market taking, we handle millions of trading transactions around the world every day, providing liquidity and ensuring competitive prices for buyers and sellers. While our presence in the market is broad, our trading desks are highly specialised, allowing for a deep understanding of unique drivers of each asset class.

Job Title: Expression of Interest - Senior Valuations Leader (non-contentious)
Ad Text:
About The Role 
 
FTI Consulting is looking for a proven Leader to join its Sydney valuations and forensics team. As a key member, you will be an integral part of this fast-growing practice.  

FTI Consulting has a dedicated team of valuation specialists providing valuations of businesses, intangible assets and many other types of financial assets for taxation, strategy, regulatory, M&A, accounting, and litigation purposes.  

A career in valuation services is exciting for many reasons. On a day-to-day basis we help our clients by analysing and assessing the value drivers of their businesses and intellectual property. Each year we interview dozens of entrepreneurs and c-suite management, and along the way, develop deep expertise in many fascinating industries.      

Some examples of the subject of our valuations: 

Shares 

Debt 

Derivatives 

Brands 

Patents and other intellectual property 

Contracts 

Infrastructure assets 

Our valuation specialists are also active in the valuation community including holding roles with local and international accounting and valuation bodies. 

 
What You'll Do 
 

Execution of a wide range of valuation engagements for strategy, transactions, regulatory, tax reporting, accounting, and litigation purposes 

Support day-to-day engagement execution, including interaction with clients, financiers, auditors, tax advisors and legal advisers 

Undertake company and industry analysis 

Participate in interviews with senior management of our clients to understand their businesses. 

Perform financial, ratio and comparable company analysis 

Perform valuations of shares, businesses, debt, intangible assets, and quantify loss and damage in commercial disputes 

Compose clear and concise written reports summarising our valuation analysis 

Assist with new business development activities 

Develop financial models and reporting templates 

Contribute to company initiatives and team development activities  

Qualifications & Experience 
 

Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related disciplines  

10+ years of professional experience, including experience in business and/or intangible asset valuations  

Strong understanding of, and interest in, valuations and corporate finance 

Experience in writing reports 

Excellent analytical and quantitative skills 

Strong knowledge of accounting principles 

Strong attention to detail and ability to handle and analyse complex financial information 

Experience in leading small teams 

Ability to work on multiple client matters at one time 

Strong work ethic and integrity, and high level of professionalism 

Willingness to travel for work 

Our Benefits
 Apart from the well-structured career path and excellent team environment, our employees enjoy a variety of perks and benefits. Our benefits include, but are not limited to:

18 weeks paid, gender-neutral parental leave with full superannuation contributions throughout paid and unpaid leave
Flexible working arrangements
Financial support to obtain work-related professional qualification, such as MRICS, ACIArb/MCIArb, and other
Professional membership contributions and generous paid study leave (up to 12 days)
Corporate discount available for health insurance, including dependent(s)
Employee Assistance Program (“EAP”) – free to all employees and their immediate families
Make an impact in our communities through company sponsored pro bono work
Special leave - 2 days per year of a cultural or religious holiday that is not treated as a public holiday in Australia
FTI Work Perks: our discount program that provides a wide range of corporate offers, savings, and wellbeing resources
Monthly Health & Wellbeing program including fitness subsidies
Annual paid volunteer hours
Corporate matching for charitable donations
Opportunity to join our Reconciliation Action Plan - Working Group; FTI’s Women’s Initiative Network (FTI WIN); Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging and Corporate Citizenship committees

About FTI Consulting
FTI Consulting is a global business advisory firm dedicated to helping clients with their most significant opportunities and challenges. With more than 7,900 employees located in 33 countries and territories, our broad and diverse bench of award–winning experts advise their clients when they are facing their most significant opportunities and challenges. At FTI Consulting, we embrace, cultivate and maintain a culture of diversity, inclusion & belonging, which are fundamental components to our core values.

FTI Consulting is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange and has been recognized as one of the World’s Best Management Consulting Firms by Forbes. For more information, visit www.fticonsulting.com and connect with us on Instagram and LinkedIn.
